title: Oxtail
excerpt: Ingredients 2 Onions, finely chopped 80 ml Garlic, finely chopped 3 Carrots, sliced 3 Celery stalks, sliced 2 Sprigs of thyme 1.5 – 2 kg Oxtail, browned nicely in a bit of oil 500 ml Old Brown Sherry 250 ml Red wine 125 ml Worcestershire sauce 20 ml Vinegar 30 ml Sugar …
html:
Ingredients

  • 2 Onions, finely chopped
  • 80 ml Garlic, finely chopped
  • 3 Carrots, sliced
  • 3 Celery stalks, sliced
  • 2 Sprigs of thyme
  • 1.5 – 2 kg Oxtail, browned nicely in a bit of oil
  • 500 ml Old Brown Sherry
  • 250 ml Red wine
  • 125 ml Worcestershire sauce
  • 20 ml Vinegar
  • 30 ml Sugar
  • 6 Cloves
  • 6 Peppercorns
  • 1 x 410 g Tin tomato puree
  • 10 Baby potatoes
  • 10 Baby onions
  • 10 Baby carrots
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • Fresh thyme to garnish

Method

Place the onions, garlic, carrots, celery and thyme in an ovenproof dish. Place the meat on top.

Mix the Old Brown Sherry, red wine, Worcestershire sauce, vinegar, sugar, cloves, peppercorns, salt, pepper and tomato puree and pour it over the meat. Cover the dish and bake at 180ºC for 3 hours.

Add the potatoes and onions. Cover again and bake for a further 1 hour before adding the baby carrots. Now bake uncovered for 1 hour. If the meat is not yet soft, extend the baking time. Season to taste with salt and pepper if needed.

As the sauce is too thin, you can thicken it with 60 ml butter and 60 ml flour melted and stirred together in a pot. Or the quickest way is to mix 80 ml Bistro into a cup of the oxtail sauce and then stir it back into the oxtail.

Garnish with fresh sprigs of thyme and serve 6 – 8 portions with polenta and roasted vegetables.

Easy!
